God gave specific instructions for observing this Passover, and, as far as we know, they did not observe another Passover until they came to Canaan. It quickly showed that the ceremonial institutions were not meant to continue forever, because shortly after they were established, some were allowed to die after many years. But the ordinance of the Lord's Supper was not set aside in the early days of the Christian church, even though those were times of greater difficulty and distress than Israel knew in the wilderness; indeed, during times of persecution, the Lord’s Supper was celebrated more frequently than later. The Israelites in the wilderness could not forget their deliverance from Egypt. There was a danger of this when they came to Canaan. Instructions were given concerning those who were ceremonially unclean, regarding when they should eat the Passover. Those whose minds and consciences are defiled by sin are unfit for communion with God, and cannot partake with comfort of the gospel Passover until they are cleansed by true repentance and faith. Observe with what concern these men complained that they were held back from offering to the Lord. It should be a concern to us when we are kept back from the solemnities of a Sabbath or a sacrament because of any circumstance. Observe Moses’ deliberation in resolving this case. Ministers must ask counsel of God's mouth, not determine according to their own feelings or preferences, but according to the word of God to the best of their knowledge. And if, in difficult cases, time is taken to spread the matter before God by humble, believing prayer, the Holy Spirit will certainly direct in the good and right way. God gave directions in this case, and in other similar cases, explaining the law of the Passover. As those who are forced against their wills to absent themselves from God's ordinances may expect the favors of God’s grace under their affliction, so those who absent themselves by choice may expect God’s wrath for their sin. Do not be deceived: God is not mocked.

This cloud was appointed as the visible sign and symbol of God's presence with Israel. Thus we are taught to see God always near us, both night and day. As long as the cloud rested on the tabernacle, they continued in the same place. There is no time lost while we are waiting for God’s timing. When the cloud was taken up, they moved, even though they were comfortably encamped. We are kept uncertain about the time of putting off our earthly house like this tabernacle, so that we may always be ready to move at the Lord's command. It is very safe and pleasant going when we see God before us, and resting where he appoints us to rest. The leading of this cloud is spoken of as signifying the guidance of the blessed Spirit. We are not now to expect such tokens of the Divine presence and guidance; but the promise is sure to all God’s spiritual Israel that he will guide them by his counsel, even unto death. All the children of God shall be led by the Spirit of God. He will direct the paths of those who in all their ways acknowledge him. At the Lord's command, our hearts should always move and rest, saying, Father, your will be done; dispose of me and mine as you please. What you will, and where you will; only let me be yours, and always in the way of my duty. In applying general precepts to particular circumstances, there should be good counsel and fervent prayer. When any undertaking is evidently wrong, or doubtfully right, and yet the mind leans that way, in such a case what people sometimes miscall “the moving of the cloud” is generally no more than a temptation Satan is permitted to propose; and people fancy they are following the Lord when they are following their own wayward inclinations. The record of his mercy will conduct us with unerring truth, through Christ, to everlasting peace. Follow the pillar of the cloud and of fire. Lay the BIBLE to heart, and receive with meekness the ingrafted word, which is able to save your souls.
